commit.message:
[ecmp] Skip ECMP balance test on dualtor topologies (#22760)

Dualtor topologies use mux cables on downstream ports. The ECMP
balance test sends traffic from downstream PTF ports expecting
direct forwarding to upstream ports, but in dualtor the mux cable
layer prevents packets from reaching the DUT correctly.

This causes consistent failures:
- dualtor-aa: 'DUT forwarded 0 packets' (0% pass rate)
- dualtor-120: 'Test results identical with/without offset' (25% pass rate)
- All t0/t1 topologies pass at 100%.

Add a topo_name parameter to is_supported() in the platform handler
classes. BroadcomPlatformHandler.is_supported() now checks if the
topology name contains 'dualtor' and returns False, causing the test
to be skipped via pytest.skip() in ECMPHashManager.

Signed-off-by: Zhaohui Sun <zhaohuisun@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add in-memory API response caching with 15-min time bucketing

commit.message:
Fix tab selection bug: read from active tab instead of cross-syncing

The old approach used observers to sync all tab selections to a single
selectedIndex property, but the 'All' tab observer would race and
overwrite selections from Arrivals/Departures tabs.

Now tracks which tab was last interacted with via activeTab property,
and reads the correct per-tab selection (selAll/selArr/selDep) when
the dialog closes.

commit.message:
Fix BLE notification packet op/vendor_id order in rf_link_slave_add_status

The refactored HEAD version incorrectly placed vendor_id (2 bytes) before
op (1 byte) in the assembled notification payload, reversing the original
mesh packet layout where op precedes vendor_id. This caused Python clients
to read the vendor_id low byte (0x11) as the opcode instead of the actual
opcode (e.g. 0x1b for STATUS).

Restore the original order: op at dat[7], vendor_id at dat[8..10], par at
dat[10..20]. This matches the raw mesh packet memory layout that the
original memcpy-based implementation produced.

Add test_rf_link_slave_add_status_packet_layout to explicitly assert the
op-first, vendor_id-second layout expected by decrypt_notification.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix race condition in time driver and related bugs

- Fix race condition in clock_time64() where reading the hardware timer
  outside the critical section allowed interrupts to update the last-seen
  timestamp in between, causing false overflow detection and an
  instantaneous multi-minute jump in Instant::now(). This manifested as
  light transitions snapping abruptly to their final state. Hardware read
  is now performed inside the critical section so the read and comparison
  are atomic with respect to interrupts.

- Fix incorrect variable read in the BLE response callback match
  expression, which was reading from the request packet instead of the
  response packet being built.

- Wrap the mesh provisioning BLE connection in an EOFError handler to
  suppress spurious errors on device reset during disconnection. Also
  reduce the post-pairing monitoring window.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix empty benchmark results: NIST CTC manifest YAML block scalar breaks custom parser (#42)

* Initial plan

* Fix empty benchmark results: convert NIST CTC manifest summary from YAML block scalar to single-line string

The custom YAML subset parser in fixture_manifest.cc does not support
YAML block scalars (> or |). The nist-ctc/manifest.yaml used:

  summary: >
    NIST MBE PMI Conformance Test Case ...

The parser reads the second indented line and throws "Unexpected
indentation", which bench_navigator catches as a hard manifest parse
error. Because HasErrors() returns true, bench_navigator exits with
EXIT_FAILURE before printing the "=== Fixture Ray Benchmark Results ==="
header. The generate_bench_report.py parser finds no header and renders
an empty page.

Fix: convert the summary to a single-line quoted string, consistent with
all other family manifests (boolean-compound, direct-primitives,
profile-faceted, tessellated, twisted-swept, wrapper-decorator).

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(destroy): fix TrailLogsBucket deletion — wrong bucket name in AWS_03 destroy

Root cause: AWS_03 destroy workflow was constructing the bucket name as
  ${PROJECT}-${ENV}-${ACCOUNT_ID}-cloudtrail
but the observability.yaml template creates it as:
  ${ProjectName}-${Environment}-cloudtrail-logs-${AWS::AccountId}
The suffix order and value were both wrong so the empty-bucket step silently
skipped the bucket, leaving objects inside, causing CF DELETE_FAILED.

Fix: get bucket name directly from the live CloudFormation stack resource
using describe-stack-resource on logical ID 'TrailLogsBucket'. This is
robust — if the bucket name ever changes in the template the destroy still
works correctly.

Also improved: handles versioned objects + delete markers in a single
loop, cleaner null checks, explicit success log message.

Re-run the AWS_03 Destroy workflow — the stack is in DELETE_FAILED state
which the workflow handles (condition is != NOT_FOUND).

New on-demand workflow that generates a 360° view of a given environment
(dev / staging / prod) as a clickable GitHub Actions Job Summary — no
AWS Console hunting required.

  • AWS_01 — VPC ID, all subnet IDs, all 6 security group IDs (clickable)

    repository URIs with direct console links
  • AWS_03 — CloudWatch dashboard link, CloudTrail link, SNS topic link,
    all 10 log group links
  • AWS_04 — Cognito User Pool link, App Client link, Hosted UI login URL
  • AWS_05 — MSK bootstrap brokers, OpenSearch endpoint + dashboard URL
  • AWS_06 — live list of all Secrets Manager secrets with direct links
  • AWS_07 — EKS cluster + node group + OIDC provider links
  • AWS_08 — ECR console shortcut (image push, no CF stack)
  • AWS_09 — All portal URLs, all API Swagger URLs, ALB + ECS links
  • AWS_10 — Lambda function link + test tab link
  • Footer — CloudFormation, CloudWatch, IAM, Cost Explorer, GitHub Actions

Output renders as markdown in the GitHub Actions Job Summary tab.

feat: implement string and type-checking function translations (#73, #74) (#110)

Add SPARQL-to-Cypher translation for:
- String functions: SUBSTR, CONCAT, REPLACE, LANG, DATATYPE
- Type-checking functions: isIRI, isBlank, isLiteral, isNumeric

SUBSTR adjusts 1-indexed SPARQL to 0-indexed Cypher substring.
REPLACE rejects unsupported 4th arg (flags).
LANG uses coalesce() for null-safe language access.
isIRI/isBlank use the isBlank boolean property from graph schema.

Includes unit tests and e2e tests for all functions.

Add SPARQL arithmetic expression translation (+, -, *, /) with proper
parenthesization, plus unary plus/minus support. The NOT IN expression
already works via E::Not(E::In(...)) composition - added a test to verify.

* style: apply rustfmt formatting

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: tighten test assertions per review feedback

- Subtraction test: check for ' - ' instead of '-' (avoids matching arrows)
- Division test: check for ' / ' instead of '/' (avoids matching URIs)
- Multiplication test: check for ' * ' instead of '*'
- NOT IN test: check for 'NOT (' and ' IN [' instead of bare 'NOT'/'IN'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add e2e tests for arithmetic and NOT IN queries

Add RDF.QUERY e2e tests that verify the full stack:
- Insert RDF data with numeric values via RDF.INSERT
- Query with FILTER(?age + 10 > 40) - addition
- Query with FILTER(?score * 2 > 170) - multiplication
- Query with FILTER(?name NOT IN ('Alice', 'Charlie'))

Tests are #[ignore] as they require FalkorDB runtime.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: render numeric/boolean/date literals unquoted in Cypher

- E::Literal now inspects datatype via oxrdf vocab constants
- xsd:integer, decimal, double, float, boolean → unquoted value
- xsd:date → date('...') function call
- Other types → quoted string (existing behavior)

This fixes arithmetic expressions like FILTER(?a * 2 > 100) which
previously produced invalid Cypher: (a * '2') instead of (a * 2).

Also makes e2e graph keys unique per test to prevent contamination.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
